description:
A researcher has a “datasets” tree under /home/user that already contains a variety of
  raw and processed data.  Your job is to create a well-structured “active” directory
  filled with symbolic links, clean up one existing broken link, create a replacement
  link, and then generate a definitive audit log so the team can quickly verify that
  everything points where it should.

  Existing material (already on disk – do NOT modify it):
    • /home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_A/            (directory)
    • /home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_B/            (directory)
    • /home/user/datasets/processed/experiment_A_clean/ (directory)
    • /home/user/datasets/processed/experiment_B_clean/ (directory)
    • /home/user/datasets/archive/exp_old              (symbolic link, currently
                                                        BROKEN – it points at the
                                                        non-existent target
                                                        “…/raw/missing_experiment”)

  What you must accomplish (you will need roughly 5–10 terminal commands):
    1. Create a new directory called /home/user/datasets/active
       if (and only if) it does not already exist.

    2. Inside /home/user/datasets/active create *exactly* four symbolic links
       with the following names and targets:
         expA_raw       →  ../raw/experiment_A
         expB_raw       →  ../raw/experiment_B
         expA_processed →  ../processed/experiment_A_clean
         expB_processed →  ../processed/experiment_B_clean
       Hints:
         • The links must be relative (as shown: they start with “..”).
         • The link *names* must match those shown above character-for-character.

    3. Remove the broken link /home/user/datasets/archive/exp_old in a way that
       leaves no dangling reference behind.

    4. Still inside /home/user/datasets/archive, create a new link:
         exp_old_to_A   →  ../raw/experiment_A
       (A relative link, again starting with “..”).

    5. Produce an audit log file called
         /home/user/datasets/symlink_audit.log
       containing one line per symbolic link that now exists anywhere under
       /home/user/datasets (search the whole tree, depth-first), **including**
       the four new links in “active” and the new link in “archive”.

       Required log-line format (exactly; one line per link, newline-terminated):
         <full_symlink_path> -> <fully_resolved_target_path>
       • <full_symlink_path> must be the absolute path to the link itself.
       • <fully_resolved_target_path> must be the canonical, absolute path that
         the link ultimately resolves to (no “..” segments).
       • Use a single space on each side of the literal arrow “->”.
       • Sort the lines in pure ASCII lexicographic order by <full_symlink_path>.
       • No extra blank lines, no headers, no trailing spaces.

    6. When finished, you should be able to run
         cat /home/user/datasets/symlink_audit.log
       and see precisely five lines that match the specification above.

  Verification notes (for your benefit):
    • The automated test will first check that /home/user/datasets/active exists.
    • It will then confirm that each of the four links in “active” exists and is
      NOT broken.
    • It will verify that /home/user/datasets/archive/exp_old has been removed and
      that /home/user/datasets/archive/exp_old_to_A is present and correct.
    • Finally, it will read /home/user/datasets/symlink_audit.log and compare the
      file’s exact byte-for-byte contents against the expected five-line list.
truth:
Final expected symbolic links (all must exist and work):

    /home/user/datasets/active/expA_processed
        → /home/user/datasets/processed/experiment_A_clean
    /home/user/datasets/active/expA_raw
        → /home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_A
    /home/user/datasets/active/expB_processed
        → /home/user/datasets/processed/experiment_B_clean
    /home/user/datasets/active/expB_raw
        → /home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_B
    /home/user/datasets/archive/exp_old_to_A
        → /home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_A

  /home/user/datasets/archive/exp_old  MUST NOT exist when the task is complete.

  Contents of /home/user/datasets/symlink_audit.log
  (exact order and exact text, each line ending in a single LF):

    /home/user/datasets/active/expA_processed -> /home/user/datasets/processed/experiment_A_clean
    /home/user/datasets/active/expA_raw -> /home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_A
    /home/user/datasets/active/expB_processed -> /home/user/datasets/processed/experiment_B_clean
    /home/user/datasets/active/expB_raw -> /home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_B
    /home/user/datasets/archive/exp_old_to_A -> /home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_A

  Preconditions (already present before the agent begins):
    Directories:
      /home/user/datasets/
      /home/user/datasets/raw/
      /home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_A/
      /home/user/datasets/raw/experiment_B/
      /home/user/datasets/processed/
      /home/user/datasets/processed/experiment_A_clean/
      /home/user/datasets/processed/experiment_B_clean/
      /home/user/datasets/archive/
    Broken link to be cleaned up:
      /home/user/datasets/archive/exp_old → ../raw/missing_experiment

  Permissions:
    Everything under /home/user/datasets is owned by the unprivileged user and is
    writable, so the agent has all required rights.
